# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.164.0/http/server.ts";
import { Request, Response } from "https://deno.land/x/oak/mod.ts";
import { permitNet } from "./permit_net.ts";

async function scrape(request: Request) {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const queryParams = new URLSearchParams(url.search);
  const urlToScrape = queryParams.get('url');

  if (!urlToScrape) {
    await response(request, { status: 400, body: "URL parameter is missing" });
    return;
  }

  try {
    await permitNet();
    const response = await fetch(urlToScrape);
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`Failed to fetch data from ${urlToScrape}: ${response.statusText}`);
    }

    const content = await response.text();
    return content;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    await response(request, { status: 500, body: error.message });
  }
}

async function response(request: Request, responseOptions: any) {
  const { status, headers, body } = responseOptions;
  const responseInit = { status, headers, body: new TextEncoder().encode(body) };

  const resp = new Response(responseInit);
  await resp.writeHeadAsync();
  request.respondWith(resp);
}

serve((req) => scrape(req), { port: 8000 });
